channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pelicans players, coach grieve those killed in New Orleans attack as they play in Miami
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Trey Murphy III knew his mother was in downtown New Orleans to ring in the new year. His chef was there as well. And when Murphy heard about the tragedy that happened on Bourbon Street, he was scared for their safety. The word eventually came: They were fine.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pistons guard Jaden Ivey sustains leg injury in victory over the Magic
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Detroit guard Jaden Ivey sustained what appeared to be a severe injury to his lower left leg in the fourth quarter of Detroit's victory over the Orlando Magic on Wednesday night. Orlando's Cole Anthony slipped and fell as he and Ivey went for the ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVE HOGG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 6 Ohio State routs top-ranked Oregon 41-21 in the Rose Bowl, advances to CFP semifinals
item.description: No. 6 Ohio State roared into the College Football Playoff semifinals with a 41-21 victory over No. 1 Oregon in the 111th Rose Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Greg Beacham, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No. 6 Ohio State routs top-ranked Oregon 41-21 in the Rose Bowl, advances to CFP semifinals
item.description: The Buckeyes scored on six of their first seven drives and took a 34-0 lead late in the second quarter on the nation’s only remaining unbeaten team.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Targeting no-call at Peach Bowl raises more questions about disputed rule
item.description: The Peach Bowl referee declined to call a targeting penalty late in the fourth quarter for a helmet-to-helmet hit by Texas safety Michael Taaffe that could’ve given Arizona State a chance to kick a winning field goal in the College Football Playoff quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Paul Newberry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Skattebo's do-it-all show is not enough to overcome Arizona State's 4th-down woes against Texas
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Cam Skattebo's do-it-all offensive show wasn't enough to save Arizona State from a stretch of fourth-down failures against Texas. Led by Skattebo, the senior running back, Arizona State compiled big advantages in rushing yards, total yards and time of possession.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: SEC commissioner speaks out on Sugar Bowl postponement amid New Orleans attack as fans, pundits weigh in
item.description: Football fans and pundits on social media were divided about the decision to postpone the Sugar Bowl in the wake of Wednesday's New Orleans attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday. Coach Sean McVay announced the decision Wednesday to rest Stafford, the 16-year veteran starter.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gauff and Fritz put US in United Cup semifinals. Kazakhstan beats Germany to advance
item.description: Coco Gauff and Taylor Fritz powered the U.S., tournament winner two years ago, into the semifinals of the United Cup mixed teams tennis event on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 49ers will start third different quarterback this season in Sunday finale at Arizona
item.description: Josh Dobbs will start in place of an injured Brock Purdy in the 49ers' season finale Sunday at the Arizona Cardinals.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS — The College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame has been postponed after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away early Wednesday, killing at least 10 people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Big 12 power rankings: Iowa State on top, West Virginia climbs (after the Kansas stunner) and Utah lands in the cellar
item.description: The Mountaineers stunned Kansas (in Lawrence) and are just behind the Cyclones in the Hotline's latest look at the Big 12 hierarchy.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: US beats Canada 4-1 to finish first in its group at world junior championship
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — Danny Nelson scored the eventual game-winner in the third period and Trey Augustine made 38 saves, leading the United States to a 4-1 win over Canada on Tuesday night and into the top spot in Group A at the world junior hockey championship.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jordan Chiles flaunts Olympic medal in New Year's post amid ongoing appeal over losing bronze to Romania
item.description: Jordan Chiles posted a photo of the bronze medal from the women's floor exercise final that she was initially awarded before it was stripped
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rangers putting goalie Igor Shesterkin on IR with an upper body injury, AP source says
item.description: New York Rangers goaltender Igor Shesterkin is going on injured reserve, according to a person with knowledge of the decision. The person spoke to The Associated Press on condition of anonymity Tuesday because the moved had not been announced. The person said Shesterkin has an upper-body injury.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Westbrook's triple-double leads Denver past Utah
item.description: DENVER (AP) - Russell Westbrook became the second player in NBA history to have a triple-double with zero turnovers and shoot 100% from the field and the free-throw line in the Denver Nuggets' 132-121 win over the Utah Jazz on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah's road winning streak ends in Seattle
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) - Jaden Schwartz had a goal and two assists for the Seattle Kraken, who pulled away in the third period for a 5-2 win against the Utah Hockey Club at Climate Pledge Arena on Monday. The loss ends Utah's seven-game road winning streak. “[Points] never come easy,” Schwartz said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USU beats San Diego State on last-second shot, 67-66
item.description: SAN DIEGO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State men’s basketball earned its first road win over a ranked opponent in over three decades Saturday afternoon in front of a sellout crowd at Viejas Arena, taking down No. 20 San Diego State 67-66.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Point and Kucherov lead surging Lightning to 6-2 win over reeling Rangers
item.description: Brayden Point and Nikita Kucherov each had a goal and two assists to lead the Tampa Bay Lightning past the reeling New York Rangers 6-2 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mavs star Luka Doncic is latest pro athlete whose home was burglarized, business manager says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Luka Doncic of the Dallas Mavericks is the latest professional athlete whose home has been burglarized. The star guard's business manager told multiple media outlets Saturday there was a break-in at Doncic's home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dodgers' Ohtani and wife will soon be welcoming a baby to the family
item.description: Between a marriage announcement, third MVP award and World Series title, Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani had a pretty good 2024. And now, it looks like 2025 may be even better for the Japanese two-way baseball phenom.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dies from cancer at age 78
item.description: Greg Gumbel, a longtime CBS sportscaster who broke barriers during his career calling some of the biggest sporting events, has died from cancer, according to a statement from his family released by the network on Friday. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago brings losing streak into matchup with Dallas
item.description: Dallas Stars (20-13-1, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (12-22-2, in the Central Division) Chicago; Sunday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks look to break their three-game skid when they play the Dallas Stars.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Southern California overcomes 17-point deficit to beat Texas A&M 35-31 in Las Vegas Bowl
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Southern California Trojans ended their season the way they started it. On Sept. 1 at Allegiant Stadium in the opener, the Trojans overcame a third-quarter deficit and scored with eight seconds left to beat LSU 27-20.
item.dc:creator: W.G. RAMIREZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Column: The path of Matthew Stafford looms for Caleb Williams
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — A skilled quarterback burdened by the dysfunction of his organization, doomed to toil in mediocrity for over a decade despite a high level of performance year in and year out.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers agree to new deal with Teoscar Hernández: report
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers fans will need to stock up on sunflower seeds after the team agreed to a new deal with fan favorite slugger Teoscar Hernández. Hernández will return to the Dodgers on a new 3-year, $66 million deal, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Bulls blow 21 point lead in 141-133 loss to Atlanta
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jalen Johnson had a career-high 30 points and the Atlanta Hawks rallied from a late 21-point deficit by scoring 50 points in the fourth quarter in a 141-133 win over the Chicago Bulls on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bears lose rain-soaked rock fight to the Seahawks, drop franchise record-tying 10th straight game
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Bears final home game of the season against the Seattle Seahawks was offense optional, as rain poured down on Soldier Field in what amounted to a cold, wet rock fight. "I thought special teams played very well.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: At least 65 million tune in for Netflix NFL Christmas Day games. NBA holiday ratings also skyrocket
item.description: LeBron James and the NBA are going to have make room for the NFL on Christmas. That shouldn't be a problem. Both leagues were winners on Wednesday. Netflix set records as the most-streamed NFL games in U.S. history while the NBA had its best holiday numbers in five years according to Nielsen.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY and TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Holiday Bowl to kick off at brand-new Snapdragon Stadium for the first time
item.description: The Holiday Bowl has been woven into the fabric of San Diego for four decades. For a football city without a professional sports team, there is even more excitement surrounding this annual event.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5
